text.title: Request Structure, Models, and Response Parsing
text.summary: Chat Completions request structure, model table with pricing, parameters reference, response parsing, and message roles.

example.code:
from openai import OpenAI
client = OpenAI() # uses OPENAI_API_KEY env var
response = client.chat.completions.create( model="gpt-4o", messages=[ {"role": "system", "content": "You are a technical writer specializing in AI."}, {"role": "user", "content": "Write a 200-word introduction about AI for developers."} ], temperature=0.7, max_tokens=4096, response_format={"type": "json_object"}, # force JSON output seed=42 ) print(response.choices[0].message.content)

list.item: gpt-4o: 128K ctx, $2.50/$10.00 per M tokens — general purpose, best quality
gpt-4o-mini: 128K ctx, $0.15/$0.60 per M tokens — cost-effective inner loops
o1: 128K ctx, $15.00/$60.00 per M — complex reasoning
o3-mini: 200K ctx, $1.10/$4.40 per M — latest reasoning model

rule.statement: Use gpt-4o-mini for agent inner loops (cheap, fast); escalate to gpt-4o only for quality gates. Never use gpt-4-turbo for new code — replaced by gpt-4o.
rule.rationale: Cost difference between gpt-4o-mini and gpt-4o is 16x on input tokens. Inner loops typically run thousands of times per pipeline run.

rule.statement: Always check finish_reason — "length" means the output was truncated by max_tokens; never parse truncated JSON blindly, detect and retry with larger max_tokens.
rule.rationale: Truncated JSON silently corrupts downstream pipeline stages; the error appears in the consumer, not at the generation site.

example.code:
response = client.chat.completions.create(model="gpt-4o", messages=[...])
choice = response.choices[0] if choice.finish_reason == "length": raise ValueError("Response truncated — increase max_tokens")
content = choice.message.content usage = response.usage print(f"Tokens: {usage.prompt_tokens} in, {usage.completion_tokens} out")

list.item: system: set behavior, persona, and output format instructions
user: user input or pipeline-injected content
assistant: model responses to include for multi-turn context
tool: tool/function results in function calling workflows
